evidence_summary: Establish the transition of power following the Witch's death by moving from the tension of captivity to a communal celebration. The visual narrative must track the physical liberation of the Lion and the subsequent political shift signaled by the Winkies'...
Dorothy frees the Cowardly Lion from his cage and announces to the Winkies that they are no longer slaves. The Winkies celebrate their liberation with a feast at the Yellow Castle.
From tension/captivity to jubilation and relief.
Dorothy stands before the locked cage of the Cowardly Lion amidst a tense atmosphere.
The courtyard is filled with Winkies celebrating at feast tables.
Dorothy releases the Lion and declares freedom to the Winkies.
